title: Vaccine Mandates Rekindle Fierce Debate Over Civil Liberties
content: Tougher requirements in some European nations have inspired pushback from angry citizens as leaders grapple with how far to go in the name of public health.
en_title: Vaccine Mandates Rekindle Fierce Debate Over Civil Liberties
en_content: Tougher requirements in some European nations have inspired pushback from angry citizens as leaders grapple with how far to go in the name of public health.

title: Turkey’s President Follows His Own Advice Even as Economy Slips
content: President Recep Tayyip Erdogan is increasingly isolated as he clings to an economic prescription that few endorse but that he believes will bear results before elections.
en_title: Turkey’s President Follows His Own Advice Even as Economy Slips
en_content: President Recep Tayyip Erdogan is increasingly isolated as he clings to an economic prescription that few endorse but that he believes will bear results before elections.

title: Migrant Truck Crash in Mexico: Disaster ‘in the Blink of an Eye’
content: The migrants, mostly from Guatemala and apparently U.S.-bound, had been crammed into a tractor-trailer that flipped and slammed into a bridge in southern Mexico.
en_title: Migrant Truck Crash in Mexico: Disaster ‘in the Blink of an Eye’
en_content: The migrants, mostly from Guatemala and apparently U.S.-bound, had been crammed into a tractor-trailer that flipped and slammed into a bridge in southern Mexico.

title: ‘Social Detonator’: In Artist’s Work, and Life, Different Classes Collide
content: Oscar Murillo, a Colombian-born painter raised in London, considers it an “infiltration” when his class-conscious canvases wind up on the walls of collectors.
en_title: ‘Social Detonator’: In Artist’s Work, and Life, Different Classes Collide
en_content: Oscar Murillo, a Colombian-born painter raised in London, considers it an “infiltration” when his class-conscious canvases wind up on the walls of collectors.

title: ‘European Sovereignty’ on the Menu as Macron and Scholz Meet for Lunch
content: The new German chancellor made his first foreign stop in Paris, where the two leaders discussed a more independent, bolder Europe.

more_info.byline: By Roger Cohen and Katrin Bennhold

en_title: ‘European Sovereignty’ on the Menu as Macron and Scholz Meet for Lunch
en_content: The new German chancellor made his first foreign stop in Paris, where the two leaders discussed a more independent, bolder Europe.

title: Modernization of Notre-Dame Interior Gets Green Light
content: A commission of heritage experts approved proposals to bring in contemporary art and move some furniture to make more space for visitors. Critics say it will debase the centuries-old church.
en_title: Modernization of Notre-Dame Interior Gets Green Light
en_content: A commission of heritage experts approved proposals to bring in contemporary art and move some furniture to make more space for visitors. Critics say it will debase the centuries-old church.

title: Tigray Rebels Executed Dozens of Civilians, Report Says
content: The report from Human Rights Watch adds to the mounting violations committed by the warring parties since the conflict in Ethiopia’s northern Tigray region began over a year ago.
en_title: Tigray Rebels Executed Dozens of Civilians, Report Says
en_content: The report from Human Rights Watch adds to the mounting violations committed by the warring parties since the conflict in Ethiopia’s northern Tigray region began over a year ago.

title: ‘Rockin’ All Over The World’ With a Diplomat’s Road Trip Music

title: Nobel-Winning Journalists Denounce War Talk and Online ‘Toxic Sludge’

title: U.K. Court Rules Julian Assange Can Be Extradited to U.S.
content: The WikiLeaks founder will seek to appeal. But if the extradition goes ahead, he would face espionage charges that could put him in prison for decades.

more_info.byline: By Megan Specia and Charlie Savage

en_title: U.K. Court Rules Julian Assange Can Be Extradited to U.S.
en_content: The WikiLeaks founder will seek to appeal. But if the extradition goes ahead, he would face espionage charges that could put him in prison for decades.

title: Taiwan Loses Nicaragua as Ally as Tensions With China Rise
content: The nation switched diplomatic allegiance to Beijing, leaving 13 nations and the Vatican still recognizing Taiwan as a sovereign nation.
en_title: Taiwan Loses Nicaragua as Ally as Tensions With China Rise
en_content: The nation switched diplomatic allegiance to Beijing, leaving 13 nations and the Vatican still recognizing Taiwan as a sovereign nation.

title: Earth Is Getting a ‘Black Box’ to Hold Humans Accountable for Climate Change
content: When a plane crashes, its flight recorder is critical to piecing together the missteps that led to calamity. Now the planet is getting its own in case it self-destructs.
en_title: Earth Is Getting a ‘Black Box’ to Hold Humans Accountable for Climate Change
en_content: When a plane crashes, its flight recorder is critical to piecing together the missteps that led to calamity. Now the planet is getting its own in case it self-destructs.

title: Omicron Brings New Round of Foreboding for British Businesses

title: U.S. Announces End to Combat Mission in Iraq, but Troops Will Not Leave
content: The U.S. military said it had transitioned to an advise and assist mission in the country, but the roughly 2,500 service members on the ground will remain, staying on in support roles.

title: Danish Leader Is Questioned Over ‘Minkgate’ Cull Driven by Covid Fears
content: The prime minister, Mette Frederiksen, said she did not know the government lacked legal authority to order the mass slaughter of 17 million minks after infected animals passed the virus to humans.

title: What Data Shows About Vaccine Supply and Demand in the Most Vulnerable Places
